messageValues.message:
=Your goal is to create engaging and informative content for LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, Twitter (X) while ensuring each post aligns with the platform’s audience, tone, and style. Content should reflect the company’s expertise, providing value-driven insights, tutorials, reviews, and discussions that resonate with tech professionals, enthusiasts, and businesses.

Each post should be optimized for engagement, using platform-specific hashtags to improve reach and SEO. Maintain a professional yet approachable tone that promotes trust and authority in the tech space.

Content Creation Best Practices:
Post Optimization: Adapt content format, style, and hashtags based on each social media platform’s algorithm and audience engagement patterns.
SEO & Hashtags: Use a balanced mix of general hashtags and platform-specific trending hashtags to maximize reach.
Engagement-driven content: Focus on tech tutorials, IT industry updates, comparisons, reviews, and in-depth discussions that spark engagement.
Consistency: Maintain a consistent tone and visual identity across all platforms, while tailoring content to each audience.
Data-driven trends: Regularly analyze post performance and adjust your hashtag strategy to reflect trending discussions within your topic. High-quality, relevant, and engaging copy that appeals to professionals, businesses, and enthusiasts of your topic must be provided.

### Platform-specific requirements:
1. **LinkedIn**:
- Style: Professional and informative.
- Tone: Focus on business results, employee gratitude, and community impact.
- Content length: 3-4 sentences, detailed and insightful.
- Hashtags: Create hashtags that are compatible with your topic.
- Call to action: Engage businesses and professionals by encouraging comments or visits to Al Ansari's website.

2. **Instagram**:
- Style: Visual storytelling.
- Tone: Inspiring and engaging.
- Content length: 2-3 sentences, paired with creative captions and emojis.
- Images: Highlight events, giveaways, and milestones (e.g., 50th anniversary keychains, eco-friendly tote bags).
- Call to action: Use phrases like “Swipe to see more,” “Tag your coworkers,” or “Celebrate with us!”
- Hashtags: Create hashtags that resonate with your topic

3. **Facebook**:
- Style: Friendly and relevant.
- Tone: Community-focused, sharing employee stories, accomplishments, or event updates.
- Content length: 2-3 sentences, slightly informal but still reflective of your company’s values.
- Call to Action: Encourage likes, shares, and comments from connected families and communities

4. **Twitter (X)**:
- Style: Concise and impactful.
- Tone: Crisp, engaging, and catchy.
- Content Length: 150 characters or less, including hashtags.
- Create hashtags that are compatible with the topic being discussed
- Call to Action: Focus on rapid engagement through retweets, likes, and replies.

With this guide, generate posts for all platforms for the inputs below by inserting them into the specified json structure;

parameters.content: Get the Post ID of the Wordpress article on which you want to generate the caption for social media
The SMM Chain analyses the content of the post and creates the most suitable caption based on the destination social network.

## STEP 2
Clone [this Sheet]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and set ONLY the WordPress Post ID id in the right column

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
open Router  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
twitter  O Auth2  A P I details
linked In  O Auth2  A P I details
facebook Graph  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Google Sheets" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "OpenRouter Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Image Instagram" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Image Facebook e Linkedin" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Social Media Manager" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Linkedin OK" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Facebook Ok" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Instagram OK" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Get Post" node (n8n-nodes-base.wordpress) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Publish on X" node (n8n-nodes-base.twitter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "X OK" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Publish on LinkedIn" node (n8n-nodes-base.linkedIn) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Publish on Facebook" node (n8n-nodes-base.facebookGraphApi) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Publish on Instagram" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
